title: cylindropuntia imbricata var. argentea
intro: Cylindropuntia imbricata var. argentea, also known as the silver cholla, is a variety of the cholla cactus, belonging to the Opuntia genus and is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cylindropuntia imbricata var. argentea include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to provide antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
